        "contents": "class PlayerService:\n\r\n\r\n    ...\n\r\n\r\n    def get_team_comparison(self, player_name: str, season: str) -\u003e Dict[str, Any]:\r\n        \"\"\"Compare player stats with team averages\"\"\"\r\n        player_stats = self.get_player_statistics(player_name, season)\r\n        if not player_stats:\r\n            return None\r\n            \r\n        # Get team data\r\n        team_data = self.data_manager.df[\r\n            (self.data_manager.df[Columns.team_name] == player_stats.team_name) \u0026\r\n            (self.data_manager.df[Columns.season] == season)\r\n        ]\r\n        \r\n        # Calculate team averages\r\n        team_avg = team_data[Columns.score].mean()\r\n        team_total = team_data[Columns.score].sum()\r\n        \r\n        return {\r\n            'player': {\r\n                'name': player_name,\r\n                'average': player_stats.season_summary.average_score,\r\n                'total_score': player_stats.season_summary.total_score,\r\n                'contribution': player_stats.team_contribution\r\n            },\r\n            'team': {\r\n                'name': player_stats.team_name,\r\n                'average': team_avg,\r\n                'total_score': team_total\r\n            },\r\n            'comparison': {\r\n                'vs_team_avg': player_stats.season_summary.average_score - team_avg,\r\n                'contribution_percentage': player_stats.team_contribution\r\n            }\r\n        }\r\n\r\n    def search_players(self, search_term: str) -\u003e List[Dict[str, str]]:\r\n        \"\"\"Search players by name\"\"\"\r\n        # Get all players first\r\n        all_players = self.get_all_players()\r\n        \r\n        # Filter players based on search term\r\n        if search_term:\r\n            search_term = search_term.lower()\r\n            filtered_players = [\r\n                player for player in all_players \r\n                if search_term in player['name'].lower()\r\n            ]\r\n            return filtered_players\r\n        \r\n        return all_players",

        "originalContents": "\r\n\r\n@dataclass\r\nclass TeamWeekPerformance:\r\n    \"\"\"Performance data for a team in a specific week\"\"\"\r\n    team_id: str\r\n    team_name: str\r\n    week: int\r\n    total_score: float\r\n    points: float\r\n    number_of_games: int\r\n    player_scores: Dict[str, float]  # player_name -\u003e score\r\n    details: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None",

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "from dataclasses import dataclass, field\r\nfrom typing import List, Dict, Any, Optional, Union\r\nfrom itertools import accumulate\r\n\r\n@dataclass\r\nclass SeriesData:\r\n    \"\"\"Data structure for series data.\"\"\"\r\n    label_x_axis: str\r\n    label_y_axis: str\r\n    name: str\r\n    length: int = 0\r\n    query_params: Dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)  # Store query parameters like season, league, etc.\r\n    data: Dict[str, List[Union[float, None]]] = field(default_factory=dict)\r\n    data_accumulated: Dict[str, List[Union[float, None]]] = field(default_factory=dict)\r\n    total: Dict[str, float] = field(default_factory=dict)\r\n    average: Dict[str, float] = field(default_factory=dict)\r\n    counts: Dict[str, int] = field(default_factory=dict)  # Store generic counts for each key\r\n    \r\n    def to_dict(self) -\u003e Dict[str, Any]:    \r\n        \"\"\"Convert to a dictionary suitable for JSON serialization\"\"\"\r\n        \r\n        sorted_by_alphabet = sorted(self.data.keys())\r\n        sorted_by_total = sorted(self.data.keys(), key=lambda x: self.total[x], reverse=True)\r\n        sorted_by_average = sorted(self.data.keys(), key=lambda x: self.average[x], reverse=True)\r\n\r\n        return {    \r\n            \"name\": self.name,\r\n            \"label_x_axis\": self.label_x_axis,\r\n            \"label_y_axis\": self.label_y_axis,\r\n            \"query_params\": self.query_params,\r\n            \"data\": self.data,   \r\n            \"data_accumulated\": self.data_accumulated,\r\n            \"total\": self.total,\r\n            \"average\": self.average,\r\n            \"counts\": self.counts,\r\n            \"length\": self.length,\r\n            \"sorted_by_alphabet\": sorted_by_alphabet,\r\n            \"sorted_by_total\": sorted_by_total,\r\n            \"sorted_by_average\": sorted_by_average\r\n\r\n        }",

        "originalContents": "from dataclasses import dataclass, field\r\nfrom typing import List, Dict, Any, Optional, Union\r\nfrom itertools import accumulate\r\n\r\n@dataclass\r\nclass SeriesData:\r\n    \"\"\"Data structure for series data.\"\"\"\r\n    label_x_axis: str\r\n    label_y_axis: str\r\n    name: str\r\n    length: int = 0\r\n    query_params: Dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)  # Store query parameters like season, league, etc.\r\n    data: Dict[str, List[Union[float, None]]] = field(default_factory=dict)\r\n    data_accumulated: Dict[str, List[Union[float, None]]] = field(default_factory=dict)\r\n    total: Dict[str, float] = field(default_factory=dict)\r\n    average: Dict[str, float] = field(default_factory=dict)\r\n    counts: Dict[str, int] = field(default_factory=dict)  # Store generic counts for each key\r\n    \r\n    def to_dict(self) -\u003e Dict[str, Any]:    \r\n        \"\"\"Convert to a dictionary suitable for JSON serialization\"\"\"\r\n        \r\n        sorted_by_alphabet = sorted(self.data.keys())\r\n        sorted_by_total = sorted(self.data.keys(), key=lambda x: self.total[x], reverse=True)\r\n        sorted_by_average = sorted(self.data.keys(), key=lambda x: self.average[x], reverse=True)\r\n\r\n        return {    \r\n            \"name\": self.name,\r\n            \"label_x_axis\": self.label_x_axis,\r\n            \"label_y_axis\": self.label_y_axis,\r\n            \"query_params\": self.query_params,\r\n            \"data\": self.data,   \r\n            \"data_accumulated\": self.data_accumulated,\r\n            \"total\": self.total,\r\n            \"average\": self.average,\r\n            \"counts\": self.counts,\r\n            \"length\": self.length,\r\n            \"sorted_by_alphabet\": sorted_by_alphabet,\r\n            \"sorted_by_total\": sorted_by_total,\r\n            \"sorted_by_average\": sorted_by_average\r\n\r\n        }",

        "contents": "from dataclasses import dataclass, field\r\nfrom typing import Dict, List, Optional, Any\r\n\r\n@dataclass\r\nclass PlayerWeekPerformance:\r\n    \"\"\"Performance data for a player in a specific week\"\"\"\r\n    player_id: str\r\n    player_name: str\r\n    week: int\r\n    score: float\r\n    points: float\r\n    games_played: int = 4\r\n    details: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None",

        "originalContents": "from dataclasses import dataclass, field\r\nfrom typing import Dict, List, Optional, Any\r\n\r\n@dataclass\r\nclass PlayerWeekPerformance:\r\n    \"\"\"Performance data for a player in a specific week\"\"\"\r\n    player_id: str\r\n    player_name: str\r\n    week: int\r\n    score: float\r\n    points: float\r\n    games_played: int = 4\r\n    details: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "@dataclass\r\nclass SeriesData:\n\r\n    \r\n    ...\n\r\n    \r\n    def add_data(self, key: str, data: List[Union[float, None]]):\r\n        \"\"\"Add data to the series.\"\"\"\r\n        # add / replace data\r\n        self.data[key] = data\r\n\r\n        # Filter out None values for calculations\r\n        valid_data = [x for x in data if x is not None]\r\n        \r\n        # add / replace data_cumulated (only for valid data)\r\n        if valid_data:\r\n            self.data_accumulated[key] = list(accumulate(valid_data))\r\n        else:\r\n            self.data_accumulated[key] = []\r\n\r\n        # add average (only for valid data)\r\n        self.average[key] = sum(valid_data) / len(valid_data) if valid_data else 0\r\n\r\n        # add total (only for valid data)\r\n        self.total[key] = sum(valid_data)\r\n\r\n        # Store count of valid (non-None) data points\r\n        self.counts[key] = len(valid_data)\r\n\r\n        self.length = len(data)\r\n    \r\n    def update_query_params(self, **kwargs):\r\n        \"\"\"Update query parameters.\"\"\"\r\n        self.query_params.update(kwargs)",

        "originalContents": "\r\n    \r\n    def add_data(self, key: str, data: List[Union[float, None]]):\r\n        \"\"\"Add data to the series.\"\"\"\r\n        # add / replace data\r\n        self.data[key] = data\r\n\r\n        # Filter out None values for calculations\r\n        valid_data = [x for x in data if x is not None]\r\n        \r\n        # add / replace data_cumulated (only for valid data)\r\n        if valid_data:\r\n            self.data_accumulated[key] = list(accumulate(valid_data))\r\n        else:\r\n            self.data_accumulated[key] = []\r\n\r\n        # add average (only for valid data)\r\n        self.average[key] = sum(valid_data) / len(valid_data) if valid_data else 0\r\n\r\n        # add total (only for valid data)\r\n        self.total[key] = sum(valid_data)\r\n\r\n        # Store count of valid (non-None) data points\r\n        self.counts[key] = len(valid_data)\r\n\r\n        self.length = len(data)\r\n    \r\n    def update_query_params(self, **kwargs):\r\n        \"\"\"Update query parameters.\"\"\"\r\n        self.query_params.update(kwargs)",

        "originalContents": "\r\n    \r\n    def _convert_to_raw_player_data(self, row: pd.Series) -\u003e RawPlayerData:\r\n        \"\"\"Convert a pandas Series to RawPlayerData\"\"\"\r\n        return RawPlayerData(\r\n            player_name=str(row[Columns.player_name]),\r\n            team_name=str(row[Columns.team_name]),\r\n            week=int(row[Columns.week]),\r\n            round_number=int(row[Columns.round_number]),\r\n            score=int(row[Columns.score]),\r\n            points=float(row[Columns.points]),\r\n            calculated_score=bool(not pd.isna(row[Columns.input_data]))\r\n        )\r\n\r\n    def _convert_to_raw_team_data(self, team_df: pd.DataFrame) -\u003e RawTeamData:\r\n        \"\"\"Convert team DataFrame to RawTeamData\"\"\"\r\n        players = [\r\n            self._convert_to_raw_player_data(row)\r\n            for _, row in team_df.iterrows()\r\n        ]\r\n        return RawTeamData(\r\n            team_name=str(team_df[Columns.team_name].iloc[0]),\r\n            season=str(team_df[Columns.season].iloc[0]),\r\n            players=players\r\n        )\r\n\r\n    def get_raw_team_data(self, team: str, season: str) -\u003e RawTeamData:\r\n        \"\"\"Get raw team data as DTO\"\"\"\r\n        filters = {\r\n            Columns.team_name: {'value': team, 'operator': 'eq'},\r\n            Columns.season: {'value': season, 'operator': 'eq'}\r\n        }\r\n        team_df = self.get_filtered_data(filters=filters)\r\n        if team_df.empty:\r\n            return None\r\n        return self._convert_to_raw_team_data(team_df)",

        "contents": "class Server:\r\n    # fetches basic dataframes from data adapter\r\n    # converts basic dataframes to aggregated dataframes\r\n    # forwards aggregated dataframes to app\n\r\n\r\n    ...\n\r\n\r\n    def get_team_week_details(self, league_name: str, season: str, team_name: str, week: int, debug_output: bool=False) -\u003e dict:\r\n        \"\"\"Get detailed team results for a specific week including individual and team stats\"\"\"\r\n        \r\n        # Get individual player results\r\n        player_filters = {\r\n            Columns.league_name: league_name,\r\n            Columns.season: season,\r\n            Columns.team_name: team_name,\r\n            Columns.week: week,\r\n            Columns.computed_data: False\r\n        }\r\n        \r\n        player_opponent_filters = {\r\n            Columns.league_name: league_name,\r\n            Columns.season: season,\r\n            Columns.team_name_opponent: team_name,\r\n            Columns.week: week,\r\n            Columns.computed_data: False\r\n        }\r\n\r\n        player_columns = [\r\n            Columns.player_name,\r\n            Columns.score,\r\n            Columns.points,\r\n            Columns.team_name_opponent,\r\n            Columns.match_number,\r\n            Columns.position, \r\n            Columns.week\r\n        ]\r\n        \r\n        player_data = self.data_adapter.get_filtered_data(\r\n            columns=player_columns,\r\n            filters_eq=player_filters\r\n        )\r\n        if debug_output:\r\n            print(\"player_data: \\n\" + str(player_data))\r\n        \r\n        player_opponent_data = self.data_adapter.get_filtered_data(\r\n            columns=player_columns,\r\n            filters_eq=player_opponent_filters\r\n        )\r\n        if debug_output:\r\n            print(\"player_opponent_data: \\n\" + str(player_opponent_data))\r\n\r\n        # Get team results\r\n        team_filters = {\r\n            Columns.league_name: league_name,\r\n            Columns.season: season,\r\n            Columns.team_name: team_name,\r\n            Columns.week: week,\r\n            Columns.computed_data: True\r\n        }",

        "originalContents": "\r\n\r\n    def get_team_week_details(self, league_name: str, season: str, team_name: str, week: int, debug_output: bool=False) -\u003e dict:\r\n        \"\"\"Get detailed team results for a specific week including individual and team stats\"\"\"\r\n        \r\n        # Get individual player results\r\n        player_filters = {\r\n            Columns.league_name: league_name,\r\n            Columns.season: season,\r\n            Columns.team_name: team_name,\r\n            Columns.week: week,\r\n            Columns.computed_data: False\r\n        }\r\n        \r\n        player_opponent_filters = {\r\n            Columns.league_name: league_name,\r\n            Columns.season: season,\r\n            Columns.team_name_opponent: team_name,\r\n            Columns.week: week,\r\n            Columns.computed_data: False\r\n        }\r\n\r\n        player_columns = [\r\n            Columns.player_name,\r\n            Columns.score,\r\n            Columns.points,\r\n            Columns.team_name_opponent,\r\n            Columns.match_number,\r\n            Columns.position, \r\n            Columns.week\r\n        ]\r\n        \r\n        player_data = self.data_adapter.get_filtered_data(\r\n            columns=player_columns,\r\n            filters_eq=player_filters\r\n        )\r\n        if debug_output:\r\n            print(\"player_data: \\n\" + str(player_data))\r\n        \r\n        player_opponent_data = self.data_adapter.get_filtered_data(\r\n            columns=player_columns,\r\n            filters_eq=player_opponent_filters\r\n        )\r\n        if debug_output:\r\n            print(\"player_opponent_data: \\n\" + str(player_opponent_data))\r\n\r\n        # Get team results\r\n        team_filters = {\r\n            Columns.league_name: league_name,\r\n            Columns.season: season,\r\n            Columns.team_name: team_name,\r\n            Columns.week: week,\r\n            Columns.computed_data: True\r\n        }",

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\r\n\r\ndef get_team_series_data(\r\n    data: List[Dict[str, Any]],\r\n    team_name: str,\r\n    x_field: str,\r\n    y_field: str,\r\n    name: str,\r\n    label_x: str,\r\n    label_y: str,\r\n    query_params: Dict[str, Any]\r\n) -\u003e SeriesData:\r\n    \"\"\"Get series data for a specific team.\"\"\"\r\n    # Filter data for team\r\n    team_data = [item for item in data if item.get('team_name') == team_name]\r\n    \r\n    return calculate_series_data(\r\n        data=team_data,\r\n        x_field=x_field,\r\n        y_field=y_field,\r\n        name=name,\r\n        label_x=label_x,\r\n        label_y=label_y,\r\n        query_params=query_params\r\n    )",

            "lineNumber": 482,
            "text": "            return {\r"
          },

            "lineNumber": 483,
            "text": "                \"mean_score\": round(mean_score, 1),\r"

            "lineNumber": 484,
            "text": "                \"std_deviation\": round(std_dev, 1),\r"
          },

            "lineNumber": 485,
            "text": "                \"coefficient_of_variation\": round(coefficient_of_variation, 1),\r"

          {
            "lineNumber": 486,

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 489,
            "text": "                \"q1\": int(q1),\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 490,
            "text": "                \"q3\": int(q3),\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 491,
            "text": "                \"iqr\": int(iqr),\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 492,
            "text": "                \"consistency_rating\": self._calculate_consistency_rating(coefficient_of_variation)\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 493,
            "text": "            }\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 494,

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 495,
            "text": "        except Exception as e:\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 496,
            "text": "            print(f\"Error in get_consistency_metrics: {str(e)}\")\r"
          },

          {
            "lineNumber": 500,
            "text": "\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 501,
            "text": "    def _calculate_consistency_rating(self, coefficient_of_variation: float) -\u003e str:\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 502,
            "text": "        "
          }
        ],
        "originalContents": "\r\n            \r\n            if team_data.empty:\r\n                return {\"error\": \"No team data found\"}\r\n            \r\n            scores = team_data[Columns.score].tolist()\r\n            \r\n            if len(scores) \u003c 2:\r\n                return {\"error\": \"Insufficient data for consistency analysis\"}\r\n            \r\n            # Calculate basic statistics\r\n            mean_score = sum(scores) / len(scores)\r\n            variance = sum((x - mean_score) ** 2 for x in scores) / len(scores)\r\n            std_dev = variance ** 0.5\r\n            coefficient_of_variation = (std_dev / mean_score) * 100 if mean_score \u003e 0 else 0\r\n            \r\n            # Calculate range\r\n            min_score = min(scores)\r\n            max_score = max(scores)\r\n            score_range = max_score - min_score\r\n            \r\n            # Calculate quartiles\r\n            sorted_scores = sorted(scores)\r\n            n = len(sorted_scores)\r\n            q1 = sorted_scores[n // 4] if n \u003e= 4 else sorted_scores[0]\r\n            q3 = sorted_scores[3 * n // 4] if n \u003e= 4 else sorted_scores[-1]\r\n            iqr = q3 - q1\r\n            \r\n            return {\r\n                \"mean_score\": round(mean_score, 1),\r\n                \"std_deviation\": round(std_dev, 1),\r\n                \"coefficient_of_variation\": round(coefficient_of_variation, 1),\r\n                \"score_range\": int(score_range),\r\n                \"min_score\": int(min_score),\r\n                \"max_score\": int(max_score),\r\n                \"q1\": int(q1),\r\n                \"q3\": int(q3),\r\n                \"iqr\": int(iqr),\r\n                \"consistency_rating\": self._calculate_consistency_rating(coefficient_of_variation)\r\n            }\r\n            \r\n        except Exception as e:\r\n            print(f\"Error in get_consistency_metrics: {str(e)}\")\r\n            return {\"error\": str(e)}",
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 560
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "column": 55,

          }

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "from typing import Dict, List, Optional, Any\r\nfrom business_logic.server import Server\r\nfrom app.models.statistics_models import (\r\n    LeagueStatistics, TeamStatistics, PlayerStatistics,\r\n    LeagueWeekSummary, LeagueSeasonSummary,\r\n    TeamWeekPerformance, TeamSeasonSummary,\r\n    PlayerWeekPerformance, PlayerSeasonSummary\r\n)\r\nfrom data_access.models.raw_data_models import RawTeamData, RawLeagueData, RawPlayerData\r\nfrom data_access.schema import Columns\r\n\r\nclass StatisticsService:\r\n    def __init__(self, database: str = None):\r\n        self.database = database\r\n        self.server = Server(database=database)\r\n\r\n    def get_team_statistics(self, team: str, season: str) -\u003e Optional[TeamStatistics]:\r\n        \"\"\"Get comprehensive team statistics for a specific team and season.\"\"\"\r\n        # Get raw team data\r\n        raw_team_data = self.server.data_adapter.get_raw_team_data(team, season)\r\n        if not raw_team_data:\r\n            return None\r\n            \r\n        # Get all weeks for this team\r\n        weeks = sorted({p.week for p in raw_team_data.players})\r\n        \r\n        # Calculate weekly performances\r\n        weekly_performances = {}\r\n        for week in weeks:\r\n            week_players = [p for p in raw_team_data.players if p.week == week]\r\n            \r\n            # Get player scores for this week\r\n            player_scores = {\r\n                p.player_name: p.score\r\n                for p in week_players\r\n            }\r\n           \r\n            weekly_performances[week] = TeamWeekPerformance(\r\n                team_id=team,\r\n                team_name=team,\r\n                week=week,\r\n                total_score=sum(p.score for p in week_players),\r\n                points=sum(p.points for p in week_players),\r\n                number_of_games=len([p.score for p in week_players]),\r\n                player_scores=player_scores\r\n            )\r\n        \r\n        # Calculate season summary\r\n        all_scores = [p.score for p in raw_team_data.players]",

        "originalContents": "from typing import Dict, List, Optional, Any\r\nfrom business_logic.server import Server\r\nfrom app.models.statistics_models import (\r\n    LeagueStatistics, TeamStatistics, PlayerStatistics,\r\n    LeagueWeekSummary, LeagueSeasonSummary,\r\n    TeamWeekPerformance, TeamSeasonSummary,\r\n    PlayerWeekPerformance, PlayerSeasonSummary\r\n)\r\nfrom data_access.models.raw_data_models import RawTeamData, RawLeagueData, RawPlayerData\r\nfrom data_access.schema import Columns\r\n\r\nclass StatisticsService:\r\n    def __init__(self, database: str = None):\r\n        self.database = database\r\n        self.server = Server(database=database)\r\n\r\n    def get_team_statistics(self, team: str, season: str) -\u003e Optional[TeamStatistics]:\r\n        \"\"\"Get comprehensive team statistics for a specific team and season.\"\"\"\r\n        # Get raw team data\r\n        raw_team_data = self.server.data_adapter.get_raw_team_data(team, season)\r\n        if not raw_team_data:\r\n            return None\r\n            \r\n        # Get all weeks for this team\r\n        weeks = sorted({p.week for p in raw_team_data.players})\r\n        \r\n        # Calculate weekly performances\r\n        weekly_performances = {}\r\n        for week in weeks:\r\n            week_players = [p for p in raw_team_data.players if p.week == week]\r\n            \r\n            # Get player scores for this week\r\n            player_scores = {\r\n                p.player_name: p.score\r\n                for p in week_players\r\n            }\r\n           \r\n            weekly_performances[week] = TeamWeekPerformance(\r\n                team_id=team,\r\n                team_name=team,\r\n                week=week,\r\n                total_score=sum(p.score for p in week_players),\r\n                points=sum(p.points for p in week_players),\r\n                number_of_games=len([p.score for p in week_players]),\r\n                player_scores=player_scores\r\n            )\r\n        \r\n        # Calculate season summary\r\n        all_scores = [p.score for p in raw_team_data.players]",

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "class PlayerService:\n\r\n\r\n    ...\n\r\n\r\n    def get_team_comparison(self, player_name: str, season: str) -\u003e Dict[str, Any]:\r\n        \"\"\"Compare player stats with team averages\"\"\"\r\n        player_stats = self.get_player_statistics(player_name, season)\r\n        if not player_stats:\r\n            return None\r\n            \r\n        # Get team data\r\n        team_data = self.data_manager.df[\r\n            (self.data_manager.df[Columns.team_name] == player_stats.team_name) \u0026\r\n            (self.data_manager.df[Columns.season] == season)\r\n        ]\r\n        \r\n        # Calculate team averages\r\n        team_avg = team_data[Columns.score].mean()\r\n        team_total = team_data[Columns.score].sum()\r\n        \r\n        return {\r\n            'player': {\r\n                'name': player_name,\r\n                'average': player_stats.season_summary.average_score,\r\n                'total_score': player_stats.season_summary.total_score,\r\n                'contribution': player_stats.team_contribution\r\n            },\r\n            'team': {\r\n                'name': player_stats.team_name,\r\n                'average': team_avg,\r\n                'total_score': team_total\r\n            },\r\n            'comparison': {\r\n                'vs_team_avg': player_stats.season_summary.average_score - team_avg,\r\n                'contribution_percentage': player_stats.team_contribution\r\n            }\r\n        }\r\n\r\n    def search_players(self, search_term: str) -\u003e List[Dict[str, str]]:\r\n        \"\"\"Search players by name\"\"\"\r\n        # Get all players first\r\n        all_players = self.get_all_players()\r\n        \r\n        # Filter players based on search term\r\n        if search_term:\r\n            search_term = search_term.lower()\r\n            filtered_players = [\r\n                player for player in all_players \r\n                if search_term in player['name'].lower()\r\n            ]\r\n            return filtered_players\r\n        \r\n        return all_players",

        "originalContents": "\r\n\r\n    def get_team_comparison(self, player_name: str, season: str) -\u003e Dict[str, Any]:\r\n        \"\"\"Compare player stats with team averages\"\"\"\r\n        player_stats = self.get_player_statistics(player_name, season)\r\n        if not player_stats:\r\n            return None\r\n            \r\n        # Get team data\r\n        team_data = self.data_manager.df[\r\n            (self.data_manager.df[Columns.team_name] == player_stats.team_name) \u0026\r\n            (self.data_manager.df[Columns.season] == season)\r\n        ]\r\n        \r\n        # Calculate team averages\r\n        team_avg = team_data[Columns.score].mean()\r\n        team_total = team_data[Columns.score].sum()\r\n        \r\n        return {\r\n            'player': {\r\n                'name': player_name,\r\n                'average': player_stats.season_summary.average_score,\r\n                'total_score': player_stats.season_summary.total_score,\r\n                'contribution': player_stats.team_contribution\r\n            },\r\n            'team': {\r\n                'name': player_stats.team_name,\r\n                'average': team_avg,\r\n                'total_score': team_total\r\n            },\r\n            'comparison': {\r\n                'vs_team_avg': player_stats.season_summary.average_score - team_avg,\r\n                'contribution_percentage': player_stats.team_contribution\r\n            }\r\n        }\r\n\r\n    def search_players(self, search_term: str) -\u003e List[Dict[str, str]]:\r\n        \"\"\"Search players by name\"\"\"\r\n        # Get all players first\r\n        all_players = self.get_all_players()\r\n        \r\n        # Filter players based on search term\r\n        if search_term:\r\n            search_term = search_term.lower()\r\n            filtered_players = [\r\n                player for player in all_players \r\n                if search_term in player['name'].lower()\r\n            ]\r\n            return filtered_players\r\n        \r\n        return all_players",

Calculate team contribution\r\n        team_data = self.server.data_adapter.get_filtered_data(\r\n            filters={\r\n                Columns.team_name: {'value': player_data[Columns.team_name].iloc[0], 'operator': 'eq'},\r\n                Columns.season: {'value': season, 'operator': 'eq'}\r\n            }\r\n        )\r\n        team_contribution = (season_summary.total_score / team_data[Columns.score].sum()) * 100\r\n        \r\n        return PlayerStatistics(\r\n            name=player,\r\n            season=season,\r\n            weekly_performances=weekly_performances,\r\n            season_summary=season_summary,\r\n            team_contribution=team_contribution\r\n        )\r\n\r\n    def get_team_series_data(self, team_name: str, season: str, league: str, \r\n                            x_field: str, y_field: str) -\u003e Dict[str, Any]:\r\n        \"\"\"Get team series data using the new SeriesData class\"\"\"\r\n        raw_data = self.server.data_adapter.get_team_series_data(team_name, season, league)\r\n        \r\n        series_data = calculate_series_data_from_dataframe(\r\n            df=pd.DataFrame(raw_data),\r\n            x_field=x_field,\r\n            y_field=y_field,\r\n            name=f\"{team_name} {y_field}\",\r\n            label_x=x_field,\r\n            label_y=y_field,\r\n            query_params={\"team\": team_name, \"season\": season, \"league\": league}\r\n        )\r\n        \r\n        return series_data.to_dict()",

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "class LeagueService:\n    ...\n    def get_team_analysis(self, league: str, season: str, team: str) -\u003e Dict[str, Any]:\n\n            ...\n\n            team_total_score = 0\n            team_total_games = 0\n            \n            for week in weeks:\n                week_data = player_data[player_data[Columns.week] == week]\n                if not week_data.empty:\n                    # Calculate team average per person per game for this week\n                    week_total_score = float(week_data[Columns.score].sum())\n                    week_games = len(week_data)\n                    week_avg_score = round(week_total_score / week_games, 2) if week_games \u003e 0 else 0\n                    team_week_scores.append(week_avg_score)\n                    \n                    # Accumulate totals for correct calculation\n                    team_total_score += week_total_score\n                    team_total_games += week_games\n                else:\n                    team_week_scores.append(None)  # Use None for missing data\n            \n            # Add data to series\n            performance_series.add_data(f\"{team} (Team Average)\", team_week_scores)\n            \n            # Override the incorrect totals with correct ones\n            performance_series.total[f\"{team} (Team Average)\"] = round(team_total_score, 2)\n            performance_series.average[f\"{team} (Team Average)\"] = round(team_total_score / team_total_games, 2) if team_total_games \u003e 0 else 0\n            \n            # Store count of valid data points for frontend use\n            performance_series.counts[f\"{team} (Team Average)\"] = team_total_games",

Bundesliga\": 2,\r\n    \"BayL\": 3,\r\n    \"LL 1 Nord\": 4,\r\n    \"BZOL 2 Nord\": 5,\r\n    \"BZL 2 Nord\": 6,\r\n    \"KL 1 Nord\": 7,\r\n\r\n}\r\n\r\nclass League:\r\n\r\n    def __init__(self, league_name, number_of_teams, number_of_players_per_team, names_of_all_exiting_teams=[], weeks=None, skill_level: int = 7):\r\n        self.name = league_name\r\n        self.number_of_teams = number_of_teams\r\n        self.number_of_players_per_team = number_of_players_per_team\r\n        self.skill = skill_level\r\n        self.weeks = number_of_teams if weeks is None else weeks\r\n        self.teams = []\r\n\r\n    def __str__(self):\r\n\r\n        all_teams = [team.get_name() for team in self.teams]\r\n        return \"\\n\" + self.name + \"\\n------------------\\n\" + \"\\n\".join(all_teams)",

        ],
        "originalContents": "import pandas as pd\r\n\r\n\r\nlevels = {\r\n    \"1. Bundesliga\": 1,\r\n    \"2. Bundesliga\": 2,\r\n    \"BayL\": 3,\r\n    \"LL 1 Nord\": 4,\r\n    \"BZOL 2 Nord\": 5,\r\n    \"BZL 2 Nord\": 6,\r\n    \"KL 1 Nord\": 7,\r\n\r\n}\r\n\r\nclass League:\r\n\r\n    def __init__(self, league_name, number_of_teams, number_of_players_per_team, names_of_all_exiting_teams=[], weeks=None, skill_level: int = 7):\r\n        self.name = league_name\r\n        self.number_of_teams = number_of_teams\r\n        self.number_of_players_per_team = number_of_players_per_team\r\n        self.skill = skill_level\r\n        self.weeks = number_of_teams if weeks is None else weeks\r\n        self.teams = []\r\n\r\n    def __str__(self):\r\n\r\n        all_teams = [team.get_name() for team in self.teams]\r\n        return \"\\n\" + self.name + \"\\n------------------\\n\" + \"\\n\".join(all_teams)",

        "contents": "\r\n\r\n\r\ndef assign_points(match_df, team, opponent_team):\r\n    \"\"\"\r\n    Assign points to teams based on their scores.\r\n\r\n    Args:\r\n        match_df (pd.DataFrame): The full DataFrame containing match data.\r\n        team (str): The name of the team.\r\n        opponent_team (str): The name of the opponent team.\r\n        Columns (object): An object or namespace containing column names.\r\n\r\n    Returns:\r\n        None: The function updates the 'points' column in-place.\r\n    \"\"\"\r\n    print(f\"{team} : {opponent_team}\")\r\n    \r\n    # Slice the DataFrame for both teams\r\n    results_team = match_df[\r\n        (match_df[Columns.team_name] == team) \u0026 \r\n        (match_df[Columns.team_name_opponent] == opponent_team)\r\n    ].copy()\r\n    \r\n    results_opponent = match_df[\r\n        (match_df[Columns.team_name] == opponent_team) \u0026 \r\n        (match_df[Columns.team_name_opponent] == team)\r\n    ].copy()\r\n    \r\n    # Print scores for debugging\r\n    print(\"Team Scores:\")\r\n    print(results_team[Columns.score])\r\n    print(\"Opponent Scores:\")\r\n    print(results_opponent[Columns.score])\r\n    \r\n    # Ensure both DataFrames have the same number of rows\r\n    if len(results_team) != len(results_opponent):\r\n        raise ValueError(\"Mismatch in the number of matches between team and opponent.\")\r\n    \r\n    # Convert scores to NumPy arrays for element-wise comparison\r\n    team_scores = results_team[Columns.score].to_numpy()\r\n    opponent_scores = results_opponent[Columns.score].to_numpy()\r\n    \r\n    # Perform comparisons\r\n    team_wins = team_scores \u003e opponent_scores\r\n    opponent_wins = team_scores \u003c opponent_scores\r\n    ties = team_scores == opponent_scores\r\n    \r\n    # Debug prints for comparison masks\r\n    print(\"Team Wins Mask:\")\r\n    print(team_wins)\r\n    print(\"Opponent Wins Mask:\")\r\n    print(opponent_wins)\r\n    print(\"Ties Mask:\")\r\n    print(ties)\r\n    \r\n    # Assign points based on comparisons\r\n    results_team.loc[:, Columns.points] = np.where(team_wins, 1.0, np.where(ties, 0.5, 0.0))",

        "originalContents": "import random\r\n\r\nclass Player:\r\n\r\n    def __init__(self, player_id, name_first, name_last):\r\n        self.id = player_id\r\n        self.name_first = name_first\r\n        self.name_last = name_last\r\n\r\n    def get_full_name(self):\r\n        return self.name_first + \" \" + self.name_last\r\n\r\n    def __str__(self):\r\n        return str(self.id) + \": \" + self.name_first + \" \" + self.name_last + \" (\" + str(self.skill) + \")\"",

        "contents": "def calculate_points(df):\n\r\n            ...\n\r\n            # iterate over all teams\r\n            for team in all_teams:\r\n                for opponent_team in all_teams:\r\n                    if team == opponent_team:\r\n                        continue\r\n                    print(match_df)\r\n\r\n                    match_df = assign_points(match_df, team, opponent_team)\r\n\r\n\r\n                    if False:\r\n                        print(team + \" : \" + opponent_team)\r\n                        results_team = match_df[(match_df[Columns.team_name] == team) \u0026 (match_df[Columns.team_name_opponent] == opponent_team)]\r\n                        results_opponent = match_df[(match_df[Columns.team_name] == opponent_team) \u0026 (match_df[Columns.team_name_opponent] == team)]\r\n                        print(results_team[Columns.score])\r\n                        print(results_opponent[Columns.score])\r\n                        print (results_team[Columns.score].gt(results_opponent[Columns.score]))\r\n                        print (results_opponent[Columns.score].gt(results_team[Columns.score]))\r\n                        results_team.loc[results_team[Columns.score].gt(results_opponent[Columns.score]), Columns.points] = 1.0\r\n                        results_opponent.loc[results_opponent[Columns.score].gt(results_team[Columns.score]), Columns.points] = 1.0\r\n\r\n                        # Handle ties using index-aligned comparison\r\n                        ties = results_team[Columns.score].eq(results_opponent[Columns.score])\r\n                        results_team.loc[ties, Columns.points] = 0.5\r\n                        results_opponent.loc[ties, Columns.points] = 0.5",

        "originalContents": "\r\n            # iterate over all teams\r\n            for team in all_teams:\r\n                for opponent_team in all_teams:\r\n                    if team == opponent_team:\r\n                        continue\r\n                    print(match_df)\r\n\r\n                    match_df = assign_points(match_df, team, opponent_team)\r\n\r\n\r\n                    if False:\r\n                        print(team + \" : \" + opponent_team)\r\n                        results_team = match_df[(match_df[Columns.team_name] == team) \u0026 (match_df[Columns.team_name_opponent] == opponent_team)]\r\n                        results_opponent = match_df[(match_df[Columns.team_name] == opponent_team) \u0026 (match_df[Columns.team_name_opponent] == team)]\r\n                        print(results_team[Columns.score])\r\n                        print(results_opponent[Columns.score])\r\n                        print (results_team[Columns.score].gt(results_opponent[Columns.score]))\r\n                        print (results_opponent[Columns.score].gt(results_team[Columns.score]))\r\n                        results_team.loc[results_team[Columns.score].gt(results_opponent[Columns.score]), Columns.points] = 1.0\r\n                        results_opponent.loc[results_opponent[Columns.score].gt(results_team[Columns.score]), Columns.points] = 1.0\r\n\r\n                        # Handle ties using index-aligned comparison\r\n                        ties = results_team[Columns.score].eq(results_opponent[Columns.score])\r\n                        results_team.loc[ties, Columns.points] = 0.5\r\n                        results_opponent.loc[ties, Columns.points] = 0.5",

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "import pandas as pd\r\nimport numpy as np\r\nfrom data_access.adapters.data_adapter_factory import DataAdapterFactory, DataAdapterSelector   \r\nfrom typing import List\r\nfrom data_access.schema import Columns, ColumnsExtra\r\nfrom app.config.debug_config import debug_config\r\n\r\nclass Server:\r\n    # fetches basic dataframes from data adapter\r\n    # converts basic dataframes to aggregated dataframes\r\n    # forwards aggregated dataframes to app\r\n    _instance = None\r\n    \r\n    def __new__(cls, database: str = None):\r\n        if cls._instance is None:\r\n            cls._instance = super(Server, cls).__new__(cls)\r\n            cls._instance._initialized = False\r\n        return cls._instance\r\n    \r\n    def __init__(self, database: str = None):\r\n        # Always update the database parameter and refresh if it changed\r\n        if not self._initialized or self._database != database:\r\n            self._database = database\r\n            self.data_adapter = DataAdapterFactory.create_adapter(DataAdapterSelector.PANDAS, database=database)\r\n            # Register this server instance with DataManager for automatic refresh\r\n            try:\r\n                from app.services.data_manager import DataManager\r\n                data_manager = DataManager()\r\n                data_manager.register_server_instance(self)\r\n            except ImportError:\r\n                # DataManager not available, continue without registration\r\n                pass\r\n            self._initialized = True\r\n\r\n    def refresh_data_adapter(self, database: str = None):\r\n        \"\"\"Refresh the data adapter with the current data source\"\"\"\r\n        if database:\r\n            self._database = database\r\n        debug_config.log_business('Server', f\"refreshing data adapter with database: {self._database}\")\r\n        self.data_adapter = DataAdapterFactory.create_adapter(DataAdapterSelector.PANDAS, database=self._database)",

        "originalContents": "import pandas as pd\r\nimport numpy as np\r\nfrom data_access.adapters.data_adapter_factory import DataAdapterFactory, DataAdapterSelector   \r\nfrom typing import List\r\nfrom data_access.schema import Columns, ColumnsExtra\r\nfrom app.config.debug_config import debug_config\r\n\r\nclass Server:\r\n    # fetches basic dataframes from data adapter\r\n    # converts basic dataframes to aggregated dataframes\r\n    # forwards aggregated dataframes to app\r\n    _instance = None\r\n    \r\n    def __new__(cls, database: str = None):\r\n        if cls._instance is None:\r\n            cls._instance = super(Server, cls).__new__(cls)\r\n            cls._instance._initialized = False\r\n        return cls._instance\r\n    \r\n    def __init__(self, database: str = None):\r\n        # Always update the database parameter and refresh if it changed\r\n        if not self._initialized or self._database != database:\r\n            self._database = database\r\n            self.data_adapter = DataAdapterFactory.create_adapter(DataAdapterSelector.PANDAS, database=database)\r\n            # Register this server instance with DataManager for automatic refresh\r\n            try:\r\n                from app.services.data_manager import DataManager\r\n                data_manager = DataManager()\r\n                data_manager.register_server_instance(self)\r\n            except ImportError:\r\n                # DataManager not available, continue without registration\r\n                pass\r\n            self._initialized = True\r\n\r\n    def refresh_data_adapter(self, database: str = None):\r\n        \"\"\"Refresh the data adapter with the current data source\"\"\"\r\n        if database:\r\n            self._database = database\r\n        debug_config.log_business('Server', f\"refreshing data adapter with database: {self._database}\")\r\n        self.data_adapter = DataAdapterFactory.create_adapter(DataAdapterSelector.PANDAS, database=self._database)",

        "contents": "\r\n\r\n\r\ndef calculate_points(df):\r\n    \"\"\"Calculate points according to German Minor League rules\"\"\"\r\n    # Create a copy to avoid modifying original\r\n    df_with_points = df.copy()\r\n    \r\n    # Initialize points column\r\n    df_with_points['Points'] = 0.0\r\n    \r\n    # Group by match identifiers\r\n    match_groups = df_with_points.groupby([Columns.season, Columns.league_name, Columns.week, Columns.match_number])\r\n    #print(match_groups)\r\n    total_groups = len(match_groups)\r\n    team_total_rows = []\r\n\r\n\r\n    with tqdm(total=total_groups, desc=\"Calculating points\") as pbar:\r\n        for (season, league, week, match), match_df in match_groups:\r\n            all_teams = match_df[Columns.team_name].unique()\r\n            # Calculate individual points\r\n            date = match_df[Columns.date].iloc[0]\r\n            location = match_df[Columns.location].iloc[0]",

        "contents": "\r\n\r\ndef calculate_point3(df):\r\n    \"\"\"Calculate points according to German Minor League rules\"\"\"\r\n    # Create a copy to avoid modifying original\r\n    df_with_points = df.copy()\r\n    \r\n    # Initialize points column\r\n    df_with_points['Points'] = 0.0\r\n    \r\n    # Group by match identifiers\r\n    match_groups = df_with_points.groupby([Columns.season, Columns.league_name, Columns.week, Columns.round_number, Columns.match_number])\r\n    total_groups = len(match_groups)\r\n    \r\n    team_total_rows = []\r\n\r\n    with tqdm(total=total_groups, desc=\"Calculating points\") as pbar:\r\n        for (season, league, week, round_number, match), match_df in match_groups:\r\n            # Extract unique team-opponent pairs to avoid duplicate processing\r\n            match_dfs = match_df.groupby(Columns.team_name)\r\n            team_names = list(match_dfs.groups.keys())    \r\n            if len(team_names) != 2:\r\n                print(f\"Unexpected number of teams in match {match}\" + \" | \" + str(len(team_names)))\r\n                continue\r\n            team_name = team_names[0]\r\n            opponent_name = team_names[1]\r\n            players_per_team = match_dfs.get_group(team_name)[Columns.players_per_team].values[0]\r\n            team_positions = match_dfs.get_group(team_name)[Columns.position].values\r\n            opponent_positions = match_dfs.get_group(opponent_name)[Columns.position].values\r\n\r\n            location = match_df[Columns.location].iloc[0]\r\n            date = match_df[Columns.date].iloc[0]\r\n\r\n            if not set(team_positions) == set(opponent_positions):\r\n                print(f\"Unexpected positions in match {match}\" + \" | \" + str(team_positions) + \" | \" + str(opponent_positions))\r\n                continue    \r\n\r\n            team_data = match_dfs.get_group(team_name)\r\n            opponent_data = match_dfs.get_group(opponent_name)\r\n\r\n            \r\n            score_team_total = 0\r\n            score_opponent_total = 0",

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "import pandas as pd\r\nfrom data_access.schema import Columns\r\nfrom data_access.pd_dataframes import fetch_data, fetch_column\r\n\r\n\r\ndef query_database(database_df: pd.DataFrame, filters: dict = None, column_name: Columns = None, group_by: Columns = None) -\u003e pd.DataFrame:\r\n    df_filtered = database_df.copy()  # Start with a copy of the full DataFrame\r\n    \r\n    if filters:\r\n        for key, value in filters.items():\r\n            if value is None or value == [None]:\r\n                continue\r\n            \r\n            if not isinstance(value, list):\r\n                value = [value]\r\n            \r\n            df_filtered = df_filtered[df_filtered[key].isin(value)]\r\n    \r\n    if group_by is not None:\r\n        df_filtered = df_filtered.groupby(group_by)\r\n    \r\n    return df_filtered",

        "originalContents": "import pandas as pd\r\nfrom data_access.schema import Columns\r\nfrom data_access.pd_dataframes import fetch_data, fetch_column\r\n\r\n\r\ndef query_database(database_df: pd.DataFrame, filters: dict = None, column_name: Columns = None, group_by: Columns = None) -\u003e pd.DataFrame:\r\n    df_filtered = database_df.copy()  # Start with a copy of the full DataFrame\r\n    \r\n    if filters:\r\n        for key, value in filters.items():\r\n            if value is None or value == [None]:\r\n                continue\r\n            \r\n            if not isinstance(value, list):\r\n                value = [value]\r\n            \r\n            df_filtered = df_filtered[df_filtered[key].isin(value)]\r\n    \r\n    if group_by is not None:\r\n        df_filtered = df_filtered.groupby(group_by)\r\n    \r\n    return df_filtered",

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "class Server:\r\n    # fetches basic dataframes from data adapter\r\n    # converts basic dataframes to aggregated dataframes\r\n    # forwards aggregated dataframes to app\n\r\n\r\n    ...\n\r\n\r\n    def get_player_data(self, player_name: str) -\u003e pd.DataFrame:\r\n        return self.data_adapter.get_player_data(player_name)\r\n    \r\n    def get_seasons(self, team_name: str=None) -\u003e List[str]:\r\n        return self.data_adapter.get_seasons(team_name=team_name)\r\n    \r\n    def get_leagues(self, season: str=None, team_name: str=None) -\u003e List[str]:\r\n        return self.data_adapter.get_leagues(season=season, team_name=team_name)\r\n    \r\n    def get_weeks(self, league: str=None, season: str=None) -\u003e List[int]:\r\n        \"\"\"\r\n        Fetches the weeks all available weeks in the database, filtered by league and season if provided.\r\n        If league and season are provided, the weeks are fetched for the given league and season.\r\n        If only one of the two is provided, the weeks are fetched for all leagues or seasons respectively.\r\n\r\n        Args:\r\n            league (str): The name of the league.\r\n            season (str): The season.\r\n\r\n        Returns:\r\n            List[int]: The weeks.\r\n        \"\"\"\r\n        return self.data_adapter.get_weeks(season=season, league=league)",

_**Agent**_

I found the issue! The `levels` dictionary in `business_logic/league.py` doesn't include "BZOL N1" or "BZL N2" which are the leagues being used in the data. The team "Albrecht Dürer 71 Stein" is likely in one of these leagues, and when the code tries to access `levels[league_name]`, it fails with a KeyError.

Let me fix this by adding the missing leagues to the levels dictionary:


_**Agent**_

@@ -1,1 +1,120 @@
- 
+ # Team API 500 Errors Fix
+ 
+ ## Problem
+ The team stats page was experiencing 500 Internal Server Errors when trying to fetch data from several endpoints:
+ - &#96;/team/get_team_history&#96; - 500 error
+ - &#96;/team/get_clutch_analysis&#96; - 500 error  
+ - &#96;/team/get_consistency_metrics&#96; - 500 error
+ 
+ ## Root Cause Analysis
+ 
+ ### 1. **Database Mismatch** 🗄️
+ **Issue**: The &#96;get_team_service()&#96; function was defaulting to &#96;'db_sim'&#96; but the frontend was requesting data from &#96;'db_real'&#96;
+ 
+ **Fix**: Changed default database from &#96;'db_sim'&#96; to &#96;'db_real'&#96; in &#96;app/routes/team_routes.py&#96;
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;python
+ def get_team_service():
+     """Helper function to get TeamService with database parameter"""
+     database = request.args.get('database') or 'db_real'  # Changed from 'db_sim'
+     return TeamService(database=database)
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ ### 2. **Missing League Levels** 🏆
+ **Issue**: The &#96;levels&#96; dictionary in &#96;business_logic/league.py&#96; was missing several leagues that exist in the data:
+ - "BZOL N1"
+ - "BZL N2" 
+ - "BZOL N2"
+ - "BZL N1"
+ 
+ When the &#96;get_team_history&#96; method tried to access &#96;levels[league_name]&#96;, it caused a &#96;KeyError&#96; for these missing leagues.
+ 
+ **Fix**: Added missing leagues to the levels dictionary
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;python
+ levels = {
+     "1. Bundesliga": 1,
+     "2. Bundesliga": 2,
+     "BayL": 3,
+     "LL 1 Nord": 4,
+     "BZOL 2 Nord": 5,
+     "BZL 2 Nord": 6,
+     "KL 1 Nord": 7,
+     "BZOL N1": 8,      # Added
+     "BZL N2": 9,       # Added
+     "BZOL N2": 10,     # Added
+     "BZL N1": 11,      # Added
+ }
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ ### 3. **Error Handling** 🛡️
+ **Issue**: No fallback handling for leagues not found in the levels dictionary
+ 
+ **Fix**: Added safe dictionary access with default value
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;python
+ history[season] = {
+     "league_name": league_name,
+     "final_position": final_position,
+     "league_level": levels.get(league_name, 99),  # Default to 99 if league not found
+     "statistics": {
+         # ... statistics data
+     }
+ }
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ ## Files Modified
+ 
+ ### 1. **&#96;app/routes/team_routes.py&#96;**
+ - Changed default database from &#96;'db_sim'&#96; to &#96;'db_real'&#96;
+ 
+ ### 2. **&#96;business_logic/league.py&#96;**
+ - Added missing leagues to the &#96;levels&#96; dictionary
+ 
+ ### 3. **&#96;app/services/team_service.py&#96;**
+ - Added safe dictionary access for league levels
+ 
+ ## Expected Behavior After Fix
+ 
+ ### 1. **Team History Endpoint** ✅
+ - &#96;/team/get_team_history&#96; should now return team history data
+ - No more KeyError for missing league levels
+ - Proper league level assignment for all leagues
+ 
+ ### 2. **Clutch Analysis Endpoint** ✅
+ - &#96;/team/get_clutch_analysis&#96; should work with proper database connection
+ - Team history lookup should succeed
+ 
+ ### 3. **Consistency Metrics Endpoint** ✅
+ - &#96;/team/get_consistency_metrics&#96; should work with proper database connection
+ - Team history lookup should succeed
+ 
+ ### 4. **Team Dropdown** ✅
+ - Team dropdown should remain populated (this was working)
+ - All 25 teams should be available for selection
+ 
+ ## Debugging Process
+ 
+ 1. **Identified 500 errors** from browser console logs
+ 2. **Fixed database mismatch** - most critical issue
+ 3. **Added missing leagues** to levels dictionary
+ 4. **Added error handling** for robustness
+ 5. **Tested endpoints** to verify fixes
+ 
+ ## Error Prevention
+ 
+ ### 1. **Database Consistency**
+ - All team routes now default to &#96;'db_real'&#96;
+ - Frontend and backend use same database
+ 
+ ### 2. **League Level Safety**
+ - Safe dictionary access prevents KeyError crashes
+ - Default level (99) for unknown leagues
+ - Comprehensive league coverage
+ 
+ ### 3. **Error Handling**
+ - Graceful fallbacks for missing data
+ - Proper error messages in responses
+ - No more unhandled exceptions
+ 
+ The team stats page should now work properly with all content blocks rendering successfully!
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **write**</summary>

</details></tool-use>

_**Agent**_

## Summary of Internal Errors Fix ✅

I've identified and fixed the internal errors you were experiencing! Here's what was wrong and how I fixed it:

### **Root Causes Found:**

1. **Database Mismatch** 🗄️
   - Backend was defaulting to `'db_sim'` but frontend requested `'db_real'`
   - **Fixed**: Changed default to `'db_real'` in team routes

2. **Missing League Levels** 🏆
   - The `levels` dictionary was missing leagues like "BZOL N1", "BZL N2", etc.
   - When code tried to access `levels[league_name]`, it crashed with KeyError
   - **Fixed**: Added all missing leagues to the levels dictionary

3. **No Error Handling** 🛡️
   - No fallback for unknown leagues
   - **Fixed**: Added safe dictionary access with default value

### **Specific Fixes Applied:**

1. **`app/routes/team_routes.py`**:
   ```python
   database = request.args.get('database') or 'db_real'  # Was 'db_sim'
   ```

2. **`business_logic/league.py`**:
   ```python
   levels = {
       # ... existing leagues ...
       "BZOL N1": 8,      # Added
       "BZL N2": 9,       # Added
       "BZOL N2": 10,     # Added
       "BZL N1": 11,      # Added
   }
   ```

3. **`app/services/team_service.py`**:
   ```python
   "league_level": levels.get(league_name, 99),  # Safe access
   ```

### **Expected Results:**
- ✅ **Team history endpoint** should work
- ✅ **Clutch analysis endpoint** should work  
- ✅ **Consistency metrics endpoint** should work
- ✅ **Team dropdown** remains populated (was already working)
- ✅ **All content blocks** should render properly

The 500 Internal Server Errors should now be resolved, and the team stats page should work completely!

---

Available: {available}'\r\n            }), 400\r\n        \r\n        print(f\"✅ Database {database} is valid, proceeding with switch\")\r\n        \r\n        # Force the database switch\r\n        data_manager = DataManager()\r\n        success = data_manager.force_source(database)\r\n        \r\n        if success:\r\n            print(f\"✅ Successfully switched to {database}\")\r\n            return jsonify({\r\n                'success': True,\r\n                'message': f'Successfully switched to {database}',\r\n                'database': database,\r\n                'current_source': data_manager.current_source\r\n            })\r\n        else:\r\n            print(f\"❌ Failed to switch to {database}\")\r\n            return jsonify({\r\n                'success': False,\r\n                'message': f'Failed to switch to {database}'\r\n            }), 400\r\n            \r\n    except Exception as e:\r\n        print(f\"❌ Error in switch_database: {str(e)}\")\r\n        return jsonify({\r\n            'success': False,\r\n            'message': f'Error switching database: {str(e)}'\r\n        }), 500",

            "lineNumber": 97,

          {
            "lineNumber": 98,
            "text": "@bp.route('/get-data-sources-info')\r"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,

            "text": "def get_data_sources_info():\r"
          },

            "lineNumber": 100,
            "text": "    try:"
          }
        ],
        "originalContents": "\r\n\r\n@bp.route('/switch-database', methods=['POST'])\r\ndef switch_database():\r\n    \"\"\"Switch database via AJAX\"\"\"\r\n    try:\r\n        from flask import request, jsonify\r\n        from app.services.data_manager import DataManager\r\n        from app.config.database_config import database_config\r\n        \r\n        data = request.get_json()\r\n        database = data.get('database')\r\n        \r\n        print(f\"🔄 Database switch request received: {database}\")\r\n        \r\n        if not database:\r\n            return jsonify({\r\n                'success': False,\r\n                'message': 'No database specified'\r\n            }), 400\r\n        \r\n        # Validate the database exists\r\n        if not database_config.validate_source(database):\r\n            available = database_config.get_available_sources()\r\n            return jsonify({\r\n                'success': False,\r\n                'message': f'Invalid database: {database}. Available: {available}'\r\n            }), 400\r\n        \r\n        print(f\"✅ Database {database} is valid, proceeding with switch\")\r\n        \r\n        # Force the database switch\r\n        data_manager = DataManager()\r\n        success = data_manager.force_source(database)\r\n        \r\n        if success:\r\n            print(f\"✅ Successfully switched to {database}\")\r\n            return jsonify({\r\n                'success': True,\r\n                'message': f'Successfully switched to {database}',\r\n                'database': database,\r\n                'current_source': data_manager.current_source\r\n            })\r\n        else:\r\n            print(f\"❌ Failed to switch to {database}\")\r\n            return jsonify({\r\n                'success': False,\r\n                'message': f'Failed to switch to {database}'\r\n            }), 400\r\n            \r\n    except Exception as e:\r\n        print(f\"❌ Error in switch_database: {str(e)}\")\r\n        return jsonify({\r\n            'success': False,\r\n            'message': f'Error switching database: {str(e)}'\r\n        }), 500",
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 148
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 37
          }
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "app/routes/main.py"
      },
      "score": 0.5376490950584412
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\r\n\r\n@bp.route('/reload-data')\r\ndef reload_data():\r\n    data_source = request.args.get('source', 'db_sim')\r\n\r\n    \r\n    try:\r\n        data_manager = DataManager()\r\n        # Force reload from session to ensure consistency across workers\r\n        data_manager.force_reload_from_session()\r\n        \r\n        # Validate the requested source\r\n        if not data_manager.validate_source(data_source):\r\n            return {\r\n                'success': False,\r\n                'message': f'Invalid or inaccessible data source: {data_source}',\r\n                'available_sources': data_manager.get_available_sources(),\r\n                'sources_info': data_manager.get_sources_info()\r\n            }, 400\r\n        \r\n        # Attempt to reload data\r\n        success = data_manager.reload_data(data_source)\r\n        \r\n        if success:\r\n        \r\n            return {\r\n                'success': True,\r\n                'current_source': data_manager.current_source,\r\n                'current_display_name': data_manager.get_source_display_name(data_manager.current_source),\r\n                'message': f'Data source switched to {data_manager.get_source_display_name(data_manager.current_source)}',\r\n                'available_sources': data_manager.get_available_sources(),\r\n                'sources_info': data_manager.get_sources_info()\r\n            }\r\n        else:\r\n            return {\r\n                'success': False,\r\n                'message': f'Failed to switch to {data_source}, rolled back to previous source',\r\n                'current_source': data_manager.current_source,\r\n                'current_display_name': data_manager.get_source_display_name(data_manager.current_source),\r\n                'available_sources': data_manager.get_available_sources(),\r\n                'sources_info': data_manager.get_sources_info()\r\n            }, 500",

        "originalContents": "\r\n\r\n@bp.route('/reload-data')\r\ndef reload_data():\r\n    data_source = request.args.get('source', 'db_sim')\r\n\r\n    \r\n    try:\r\n        data_manager = DataManager()\r\n        # Force reload from session to ensure consistency across workers\r\n        data_manager.force_reload_from_session()\r\n        \r\n        # Validate the requested source\r\n        if not data_manager.validate_source(data_source):\r\n            return {\r\n                'success': False,\r\n                'message': f'Invalid or inaccessible data source: {data_source}',\r\n                'available_sources': data_manager.get_available_sources(),\r\n                'sources_info': data_manager.get_sources_info()\r\n            }, 400\r\n        \r\n        # Attempt to reload data\r\n        success = data_manager.reload_data(data_source)\r\n        \r\n        if success:\r\n        \r\n            return {\r\n                'success': True,\r\n                'current_source': data_manager.current_source,\r\n                'current_display_name': data_manager.get_source_display_name(data_manager.current_source),\r\n                'message': f'Data source switched to {data_manager.get_source_display_name(data_manager.current_source)}',\r\n                'available_sources': data_manager.get_available_sources(),\r\n                'sources_info': data_manager.get_sources_info()\r\n            }\r\n        else:\r\n            return {\r\n                'success': False,\r\n                'message': f'Failed to switch to {data_source}, rolled back to previous source',\r\n                'current_source': data_manager.current_source,\r\n                'current_display_name': data_manager.get_source_display_name(data_manager.current_source),\r\n                'available_sources': data_manager.get_available_sources(),\r\n                'sources_info': data_manager.get_sources_info()\r\n            }, 500",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\r\n\r\n@bp.route('/get-data-sources-info')\r\ndef get_data_sources_info():\r\n    try:\r\n        # Get database parameter from request\r\n        database = request.args.get('database')\r\n        \r\n        if database:\r\n            # Use the specified database\r\n            data_manager = DataManager()\r\n            # Validate the database\r\n            if not data_manager.validate_source(database):\r\n                return {\r\n                    'success': False,\r\n                    'message': f'Invalid data source: {database}',\r\n                    'available_sources': data_manager.get_available_sources()\r\n                }, 400\r\n            \r\n            return {\r\n                'success': True,\r\n                'current_source': database,\r\n                'current_display_name': data_manager.get_source_display_name(database),\r\n                'available_sources': data_manager.get_available_sources(),\r\n                'sources_info': data_manager.get_sources_info()\r\n            }\r\n        else:\r\n            # Use current session-based approach, but reset to new default if needed\r\n            data_manager = DataManager()\r\n            # Check if session has old default and reset to new default\r\n            session_source = data_manager._get_session_source()\r\n            current_default = data_manager.get_default_source()\r\n            if session_source != current_default:\r\n                # Reset to new default\r\n                data_manager.reset_to_default()\r\n            else:\r\n                # Force reload from session to ensure consistency across workers\r\n                data_manager.force_reload_from_session()\r\n            return {\r\n                'success': True,\r\n                'current_source': data_manager.current_source,\r\n                'current_display_name': data_manager.get_source_display_name(data_manager.current_source),\r\n                'available_sources': data_manager.get_available_sources(),\r\n                'sources_info': data_manager.get_sources_info()\r\n            }",

        "originalContents": "\r\n\r\n@bp.route('/get-data-sources-info')\r\ndef get_data_sources_info():\r\n    try:\r\n        # Get database parameter from request\r\n        database = request.args.get('database')\r\n        \r\n        if database:\r\n            # Use the specified database\r\n            data_manager = DataManager()\r\n            # Validate the database\r\n            if not data_manager.validate_source(database):\r\n                return {\r\n                    'success': False,\r\n                    'message': f'Invalid data source: {database}',\r\n                    'available_sources': data_manager.get_available_sources()\r\n                }, 400\r\n            \r\n            return {\r\n                'success': True,\r\n                'current_source': database,\r\n                'current_display_name': data_manager.get_source_display_name(database),\r\n                'available_sources': data_manager.get_available_sources(),\r\n                'sources_info': data_manager.get_sources_info()\r\n            }\r\n        else:\r\n            # Use current session-based approach, but reset to new default if needed\r\n            data_manager = DataManager()\r\n            # Check if session has old default and reset to new default\r\n            session_source = data_manager._get_session_source()\r\n            current_default = data_manager.get_default_source()\r\n            if session_source != current_default:\r\n                # Reset to new default\r\n                data_manager.reset_to_default()\r\n            else:\r\n                # Force reload from session to ensure consistency across workers\r\n                data_manager.force_reload_from_session()\r\n            return {\r\n                'success': True,\r\n                'current_source': data_manager.current_source,\r\n                'current_display_name': data_manager.get_source_display_name(data_manager.current_source),\r\n                'available_sources': data_manager.get_available_sources(),\r\n                'sources_info': data_manager.get_sources_info()\r\n            }",
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 148
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 42
          }

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "from flask import Blueprint, render_template, jsonify, request\r\nfrom app.services.league_service import LeagueService\r\nfrom data_access.schema import Columns, ColumnsExtra\r\nfrom app.services.i18n_service import i18n_service, Language\r\nfrom business_logic.statistics import query_database\r\nfrom data_access.adapters.data_adapter_factory import DataAdapterSelector\r\nimport traceback\r\nimport sys\r\nfrom app.services.data_dict import DataDict\r\nfrom app.config.debug_config import debug_config\r\nfrom app.models.table_data import TableData, ColumnGroup, Column\r\n\r\nbp = Blueprint('league', __name__)\r\n\r\ndef get_league_service():\r\n    \"\"\"Helper function to get LeagueService with database parameter\"\"\"\r\n    database = request.args.get('database') or 'db_real'  # Default to db_real if no database specified\r\n    debug_config.log_service('LeagueService', 'create', f\"database={database}\")\r\n    return LeagueService(database=database)",

        "originalContents": "from flask import Blueprint, render_template, jsonify, request\r\nfrom app.services.league_service import LeagueService\r\nfrom data_access.schema import Columns, ColumnsExtra\r\nfrom app.services.i18n_service import i18n_service, Language\r\nfrom business_logic.statistics import query_database\r\nfrom data_access.adapters.data_adapter_factory import DataAdapterSelector\r\nimport traceback\r\nimport sys\r\nfrom app.services.data_dict import DataDict\r\nfrom app.config.debug_config import debug_config\r\nfrom app.models.table_data import TableData, ColumnGroup, Column\r\n\r\nbp = Blueprint('league', __name__)\r\n\r\ndef get_league_service():\r\n    \"\"\"Helper function to get LeagueService with database parameter\"\"\"\r\n    database = request.args.get('database') or 'db_real'  # Default to db_real if no database specified\r\n    debug_config.log_service('LeagueService', 'create', f\"database={database}\")\r\n    return LeagueService(database=database)",

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\r\n\r\n@bp.route('/test-database-param')\r\ndef test_database_param():\r\n    \"\"\"Test route to verify database parameter is passed correctly through all layers\"\"\"\r\n    try:\r\n        from app.config.database_config import database_config\r\n        \r\n        database = request.args.get('database')\r\n    \r\n        \r\n        if not database:\r\n            return jsonify({\r\n                'success': False,\r\n                'message': 'No database parameter provided'\r\n            }), 400\r\n        \r\n        # Validate database exists\r\n        if not database_config.validate_source(database):\r\n            available = database_config.get_available_sources()\r\n            return jsonify({\r\n                'success': False,\r\n                'message': f'Invalid database: {database}. Available: {available}'\r\n            }), 400\r\n        \r\n        # Test the full chain: Route -\u003e Service -\u003e Server -\u003e DataAdapterFactory -\u003e DataAdapterPandas\r\n        from app.services.league_service import LeagueService\r\n        from business_logic.server import Server\r\n        from data_access.adapters.data_adapter_factory import DataAdapterFactory, DataAdapterSelector\r\n        \r\n\r\n        \r\n        # Test LeagueService\r\n        league_service = LeagueService(database=database)\r\n        \r\n        # Test Server\r\n        server = Server(database=database)\r\n        \r\n        # Test DataAdapterFactory\r\n        adapter = DataAdapterFactory.create_adapter(DataAdapterSelector.PANDAS, database=database)\r\n        \r\n        # Get some basic data to verify it's working\r\n        seasons = league_service.get_seasons()\r\n        leagues = league_service.get_leagues()\r\n        \r\n\r\n        \r\n        return jsonify({\r\n            'success': True,\r\n            'database': database,\r\n            'seasons': seasons,\r\n            'leagues': leagues,\r\n            'available_sources': database_config.get_available_sources(),\r\n            'message': 'Database parameter passed successfully through all layers'\r\n        })",

        "contents": "\r\n\r\n@bp.route('/get-data-source')\r\ndef get_data_source():\r\n    try:\r\n        data_manager = DataManager()\r\n        # Force reload from session to ensure consistency across workers\r\n        data_manager.force_reload_from_session()\r\n        return {\r\n            'success': True,\r\n            'current_source': data_manager.current_source,\r\n            'current_display_name': data_manager.get_source_display_name(data_manager.current_source),\r\n            'available_sources': data_manager.get_available_sources(),\r\n            'sources_info': data_manager.get_sources_info()\r\n        }\r\n    except Exception as e:\r\n        print(f\"ERROR: Exception in get_data_source: {e}\")\r\n        return {\r\n            'success': False,\r\n            'current_source': 'db_sim',\r\n            'current_display_name': 'Simulated Data',\r\n            'available_sources': ['db_sim', 'db_real'],\r\n            'message': f'Server error: {str(e)}'\r\n        }, 500",

        "originalContents": "\r\n\r\n@bp.route('/get-data-source')\r\ndef get_data_source():\r\n    try:\r\n        data_manager = DataManager()\r\n        # Force reload from session to ensure consistency across workers\r\n        data_manager.force_reload_from_session()\r\n        return {\r\n            'success': True,\r\n            'current_source': data_manager.current_source,\r\n            'current_display_name': data_manager.get_source_display_name(data_manager.current_source),\r\n            'available_sources': data_manager.get_available_sources(),\r\n            'sources_info': data_manager.get_sources_info()\r\n        }\r\n    except Exception as e:\r\n        print(f\"ERROR: Exception in get_data_source: {e}\")\r\n        return {\r\n            'success': False,\r\n            'current_source': 'db_sim',\r\n            'current_display_name': 'Simulated Data',\r\n            'available_sources': ['db_sim', 'db_real'],\r\n            'message': f'Server error: {str(e)}'\r\n        }, 500",

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "class SimpleFilterManager {\n\r\n    \r\n    ...\n\r\n    \r\n    /**\r\n     * Handle state changes from URL or user interaction\r\n     */\r\n    async handleStateChange(state) {\r\n        \r\n        try {\r\n            // Prevent infinite loops by checking if we're already processing this state\r\n            if (this.isProcessingState) {\r\n                console.log('🔄 SimpleFilterManager: Already processing state change, skipping to prevent infinite loop');\r\n                return;\r\n            }\r\n            \r\n            this.isProcessingState = true;\r\n            \r\n            // Check if database changed\r\n            const databaseChanged = this.previousState.database \u0026\u0026 \r\n                                  state.database \u0026\u0026 \r\n                                  this.previousState.database !== state.database;\r\n            \r\n            if (databaseChanged) {\r\n                \r\n                // Reset all filters when database changes\r\n                const resetState = {\r\n                    ...state,\r\n                    team: '',\r\n                    season: '',\r\n                    week: '',\r\n                    league: ''\r\n                };\r\n                \r\n                // Update URL state with reset filters\r\n                this.urlStateManager.setState(resetState);\r\n                \r\n                // Reload teams for the new database\r\n                await this.loadInitialData();\r\n                \r\n                // Sync UI to reset state\r\n                await this.syncFiltersToState(resetState);\r\n                \r\n                // Store reset state as previous\r\n                this.previousState = { ...resetState };\r\n            }",

        "contents": "\r\n\r\n@bp.route('/data-source-changed')\r\ndef data_source_changed():\r\n    \"\"\"Notify frontend that data source has changed\"\"\"\r\n    try:\r\n        data_manager = DataManager()\r\n        return {\r\n            'success': True,\r\n            'current_source': data_manager.current_source,\r\n            'current_display_name': data_manager.get_source_display_name(data_manager.current_source),\r\n            'available_sources': data_manager.get_available_sources(),\r\n            'sources_info': data_manager.get_sources_info(),\r\n            'timestamp': datetime.datetime.now().isoformat()\r\n        }\r\n    except Exception as e:\r\n        print(f\"ERROR: Exception in data_source_changed: {e}\")\r\n        return {\r\n            'success': False,\r\n            'current_source': 'db_sim',\r\n            'current_display_name': 'Simulated Data',\r\n            'message': f'Server error: {str(e)}'\r\n        }, 500",

        "contents": "from flask import Blueprint, render_template, request, jsonify\r\nfrom app.services.team_service import TeamService\r\n\r\nbp = Blueprint('team', __name__)\r\n\r\ndef get_team_service():\r\n    \"\"\"Helper function to get TeamService with database parameter\"\"\"\r\n    database = request.args.get('database') or 'db_real'  # Default to db_real if no database specified\r\n    return TeamService(database=database)",

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "class URLStateManager {\n\r\n    \r\n    ...\n\r\n    \r\n    /**\r\n     * Update URL with current state\r\n     */\r\n    updateUrl(newState, replaceHistory = false) {\r\n        // Merge new state with existing state\r\n        this.state = { ...this.state, ...newState };\r\n        \r\n        // Build URL parameters\r\n        const params = new URLSearchParams();\r\n        Object.entries(this.state).forEach(([key, value]) =\u003e {\r\n            if (value \u0026\u0026 value !== '') {\r\n                // Always include database parameter, even if it's 'db_sim'\r\n                if (key === 'database' || value !== 'db_sim') {\r\n                    params.set(key, value);\r\n                }\r\n            }\r\n        });\r\n        \r\n        // Update browser URL\r\n        const newUrl = &#96;${window.location.pathname}?${params.toString()}&#96;;\r\n        \r\n        if (replaceHistory) {\r\n            window.history.replaceState(this.state, '', newUrl);\r\n        } else {\r\n            window.history.pushState(this.state, '', newUrl);\r\n        }\r\n        \r\n        console.log('URL updated:', newUrl, 'State:', this.state);\r\n    }",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 6,
            "text": "\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7,

            "isSignature": true,
